evals.prompt: I want to build a recipe manager that pulls recipes from URLs and organizes them by cuisine type. I cook a lot and I'm tired of bookmarking recipes across 10 different sites and losing them. I want something self-hosted that I can run on my home server.
evals.expected_output: Should conduct discovery interview before writing documents. PRD should cover recipe URL scraping, cuisine categorization, and self-hosted deployment. TRD should propose a sensible stack with containerized deployment given the self-hosted requirement.
assertions: PRD follows numbered heading structure (sections 1-4 minimum)
TRD specifies concrete libraries with versions

evals.prompt: I need a dashboard that monitors my Docker containers and shows their logs and resource usage. Something simple I can pop open in a browser to see what's running, how much memory each container is eating, and tail logs without opening a terminal.
evals.expected_output: Should ask clarifying questions about scope (single host vs multi-host, alerting needs, auth). PRD should define the monitoring features clearly. TRD should address Docker API integration and real-time log streaming.
assertions: PRD follows numbered heading structure (sections 1-4 minimum)
TRD specifies concrete libraries with versions

evals.prompt: I want to make a thing that helps me track my reading list
evals.expected_output: Should NOT just start writing docs from this vague prompt. Should ask multiple rounds of questions: what kind of reading (books, articles, papers)? What does 'track' mean (progress, ratings, notes, recommendations)? Web app, CLI, mobile? Any integrations (Goodreads, library APIs)?
assertions: PRD follows numbered heading structure (sections 1-4 minimum)
TRD specifies concrete libraries with versions
